item.title: About
item.content:encoded:
We specialize in experiential design and event production, pushing the limits of creativity and technology to craft inspiring, unforgettable stories for our audience.

About Us

  • Who are we

    We are a team of imaginative storytellers who use technology to bring worlds to life. Our goal is to evoke emotions in our audience and create unforgettable experiences that take you on an exciting and informative journey. Our diverse team of creators, engineers, designers, architects, and technologists work together to turn even the most unconventional ideas into reality.

  • What we do

    Our services cover everything from experiential design and creative technology to specialized production and custom fabrication. We believe that any idea is achievable, and we are always experimenting with new tools and solutions to bring the best possible results to our clients. Whether it's a narrative or strategy, multimedia design or custom builds, our goal is always to create work that exceeds expectations.

  • How we do it

    When we are on your team, collaboration is key. We value open and collaborative relationships with our clients and partners, working together to make great work that inspires wonder and human connection. We are passionate about what we do, and we are always pushing the limits of what is possible. Join us in creating something truly amazing.

Tech Capabilities

Subscribe to Our Newsletter

Sign up with your email address to receive news and updates.

Thank you!

item.title: Contact
item.content:encoded:
We love to collaborate and amplify your voice, we put your message at the center of the story, lets build something awesome together.

CONTACT US

item.content:encoded: img-63d8b31d6c58e6693e58edde
item.dc:creator: studiophile@gmail.com

item.title: Projekt X: Stage Design and Fabrication
item.content:encoded:
Projekt X

CLIENT: Adriatique

LOCATION: Zurich, Switzerland

TYPE: CUSTOM FABRICATED STAGE ELEMENT

Design Objective: The goal of this project was to create a custom fabricated set piece for Adriatique's upcoming worldwide tour, "X Future," that would serve as the central element in the stage design. The set piece was designed to be easily crated, toured, rigged, pixel mapped, laser mapped, and flown in any orientation.

Design Concept: The custom-fabricated set piece was engineered to meet the highest safety standards in California, United States and shipped with all appropriate drawings and load specifications and engineering stamps. The set piece was fabricated using custom edge-lit DMX addressable LEDs that outline and are recessed inside of the structural arms of the X. The highly detailed build drawings were concepted in Rhino3D and finalized in Solid Works. The X is composed of 4 identical armatures and an interchangeable exterior skinning system, allowing for maximum modularity and flexibility. The set piece was built from aluminum to maximize the strength to weight ratio, with extensive rigging points available on each face to allow for unlimited rigging options.

Type: Custom Scenic, Fabrication, Stage Design, Concert, Event Production

Techniques: Projection Mapping, Interactive Software Development, Lighting Design, Environmental Design, Custom Fabrication, 3D scanning, Holographic Displays, 3D World Creation.

Materials:  Aluminum,  DMX addressable LEDs,  Silicone Differ, Hardened Steel

Partners: SETUP.DESIGN

item.title: Aku World: Immersive Environment
item.content:encoded:
AKU WORLD

CLIENT: AKU+FTX

LOCATION:NEW YORK, NY

TYPE: NFT MIAMI Experiential Event

Design Objectives: Create a real-world space for the NFT Miami event that brings the AKU brand to life, featuring a projection-mapped environment in the main room, an interactive subway lobby, a merch room with 3D holographic displays, and a body scanner for creating and minting digital avatars. Host panel discussions, live music performances, and a patio bar for attendees. Custom fabrication of a large-scale helmet installation, as well as exterior signage, was also included in the project.

Design Concept: The AKU World: Experiential Event is centered around the character of Aku, a digital explorer on a mission to prove that no dream is too big and no obstacle is too large. Guests are invited to explore the three virtual worlds created for the experience, including the opportunity to control the helmet installation, choose their "Inner Astronaut," and create and mint their own digital avatar through a 4D scan. The projections and lighting in the space are also linked to the musical performances, creating a fully immersive and dynamic experience.

Type: Immersive Environment, Experiential Event

Tech: Projection Mapping, Interactive Software Development, Lighting Design, Environmental Design, Custom Fabrication, 3D scanning, Holographic Displays, 3D World Creation.

Spec: Projectors, Blender, Touch Designer, Figma, Unreal Engine, Tiled TV Displays, Portal Displays, Custom Scenic Fabrication, LED Lighting

Partners: PHNTM, MATTE, imagine{X]

item.title: MyShelter: Immersive Enviroment
item.content:encoded:
MY SHELTER

CLIENT: ADIDAS

LOCATION: New York, NY

TYPE: Experiential Event

Design Objective: The goal of this project was to create an experiential immersive environment for the product launch of Adidas Outdoor's MyShelter jacket. The objective was to give people an environment to experience the jacket in the setting where it is best suited.

Design Concept: The environment was created using projection mapping to transform the walls into a lush inner forest scene. Spatial audio was used to recreate the immersive sounds of the inner forest canopy, and low-lying fog was used to create a sense of discovery and intrigue. The project also utilized audio-reactive lighting, scenic elements hung from the roof, and custom signage to create a surreal scene to keep people entertained while they enjoyed the new Adidas jacket. The projector was short-throw and hidden within the forest canopy. The projection content was a slowly animating forest scene using forced perspective to make visitors feel like they were slowly floating through a peaceful forest canopy.

Design Challenge: The space was a small and narrow shut down bodega, previously used for storing old kitchen equipment. The main challenge was to create an immersive experience within the small and narrow space.

Type: Environmental Design, Immersive Experience, Product Launch, Retail Build

Techniques: Scenic fabrication, interactive software development, projection mapping, lighting design, custom signage

Materials: Foliage, street signs and lights, CNC acrylic, addressable LEDs, TouchDesigner

Partners: Kamp Grizzly

item.title: Work
item.content:encoded:
Our creative technology projects range from permanent installations to touring events, we scale from the small details to international logistics to meet your needs.

Work

item.title: Destination: Experiential Event
item.content:encoded:
DESTINATION

CLIENT: Journey’s x ADIDAS

LOCATION: Horton Plaza Park - San Diego, CA

TYPE: Experiential Event

Created for Journey’s and ADIDAS, this 40’ tall structure was skinned with a blank canvas and outfitted with a custom built paint distribution device.   Each participant was given the chance to control the robotic paint dripper and release 5 different colors of paint.   Participants simply had to press a button for their selections and auto magically a paint would begin flowing.  

Type: Interactive Art Canvas, Custom Scenic, Fabrication, Stage Design, Concert, Event Production

Tech: Custom Controller, Firmware, Relays,  Mechanical Valves

Software: Arduino, Touchdesigner

Partners: Starch Creative

item.title: Create Our Future: Experiential Event
item.content:encoded:
CREATE OUR FUTURE

CLIENT: Urban Outfitters x ADIDAS

LOCATION: Mack Sennett Studio - LA, CA

TYPE: Experiential Event

An Experiential Event created for Adidas and Urban outfitters that featured Projection Mapped 270 Degree Cyclone Wall as a backdrop for a stage with multiple artist performances including Phantogram. Guests were also able share and experience a custom made VR experience where they could draw and customize 3D digital avatars that were captured from a Kinect Camera.

Type: Stage Design, Concert, Event Production, VR Experience, Lighting Design

Tech: Projection Mapping, Self climbing truss, Cyclone Wall, VR Headsets

Software: Unity, Touchdesigner

Partners: Round House, DotDotDash

item.title: Team
item.content:encoded:
We believe in the spirit of collaboration and foster positivity. We are agile and ready to respond with an established network of industry partners.

Our Team

  • Joey Paulekas - Touring and Fabrication

    Whether it’s Tour Manager, Art Director, Scenic Carpenter, Production Manager, Joey has done it all, He has worked for global brands and has toured the world with acts like Odesza, and Emancipator.

  • Nathan Jenkins - Creative Technologist

    Founder and CEO of Beatrox, With over 20 years of experience in the industry, Audio Visual Design and Integration, Technical Director, Lighting Designer, Sound Designer

  • Eric Mintzer - Creative Technologist

    Technologist and music lover that has developed award-winning systems for projection mapping, sensor automation, and media control. Past companies include Startup Shell, Pamble, and current founder and CEO of ImagineX.

  • Alan Curtiss - Video Production & LED Technician

    Owner of Sky Lites Drone Shows, and a staple member of the Beatrox team, Alan has been producing and managing high profile technical productions for many years with a wide variety of skills he has master the field of video production either behind the camera or playing on the screens. Alan has the skills to run many aspects of Video Production, Drone Operations, VJ/Media Servers operator, and LED Technician.

  • Desiree Daggy - 3D & Environmental Design

    Desiree is a 3D animator & Environmental Designer from Character rigging to real-time environments. Dezi has the skills to get the job done with many years of experience working the in the events and entertainment industries.

  • Joshua Vallejos - Audio Engineer / Production Manager

    Joshua Vallejos is an Audio Engineer with over 15 years of experience working as a touring and live sound engineer, studio engineer, music producer, mix & master engineer, and sound designer for games, and TV commercials. Weather on-stage or in the studio, Josh will get the job done.

  • Bryant Place - Creative Technologist

    With over 13 years of global experience in the live entertainment and permanent installation industries, Bryant has a keen focus on merging technology and art.

item.title: Buzzfeed Newfronts: Event production
item.content:encoded:
NEW FRONTS

CLIENT: Buzzfeed

LOCATION: Moynihan Station, New York, NY

TYPE: Event Production

Design Objective: To create a memorable and engaging event experience for guests at BuzzFeed's 2016 NewFronts, showcasing BuzzFeed's creative process for creating content and demonstrating their ability to meet the needs of their audience. The event space was designed with a purposeful flow and highlighted with large projection screens and individually mapped orbs, creating a visually stunning atmosphere that captivated attendees.

Design Concept: The event aimed to highlight BuzzFeed's ability to feed the hearts, minds, and businesses of their audience by showcasing their creative process through a unique and immersive event experience. The main presentation took place on a custom built stage, which incorporated projection mapping, multimedia content production, pre visualization, interactive programming and custom fabrication to bring the event to life.

Type: Event Production, Multimedia Design, Stage Design, Lighting Design

Techniques:  Projection Mapping, Pre-production, Custom Fabrication, Media Server Programming, Lighting Programming

Partners:  DotDotDash

item.title: Run For The Oceans: Interactive Media Experience
item.content:encoded:
RUN FOR THE OCEANS

CLIENT: Adidas

LOCATION:Brooklyn, NY

TYPE: Experiential Event

Design Objectives: To create an interactive and educational experience for attendees of the Parley Ocean School x Adidas annual 5k marathon event. The goal was to raise awareness about the impact of plastic waste in the ocean, while also providing an immersive and entertaining experience.

Design Concepts: A custom shipping container build was used to create an immersive environment that resembled a deep chamber within the ocean. The container was fitted with seamless wall-to-wall video panels and mirror surfaces for the floor and ceiling to create an infinity mirror effect. The interactive experience used scanning LIDAR sensors to track the participants' movements and touch interactions, and this data was used to drive a real-time visualization of flocking fish and a fluid simulation. A collage of information and footage about the deep ocean and plastic waste was also displayed on the screens.

Type: Interactive Media Experience, Event Production, Marathon, Infinity Mirror

Tech: LED Video Panel, Scanning Lidar Sensors, Custom Shipping Container Build, Flocking Simulation, Fluids Simulation, Realtime, Interactive Programming

Spec: TouchDesigner, Hokuyo, ROE Black Pearl

Location: Domino Park - Brooklyn, NY

Partners: Parley Ocean School, Adidas, Kamp Grizzly

Interactive Infinity & Media Tunnel

Event Production

item.title: Super Bowl 2020: Projection Mapping & Interactive AR
item.content:encoded:
INTERACTIVE AR & PROJECTION MAPPING

CLIENT: ADIDAS

LOCATION:MIAMI, FL

TYPE: SUPER BOWL 2020 Experiential Event

Design Objectives: To create a HQ for ADIDAS at the Super Bowl 2020 in Miami that would be able to host a VIP event for sponsored athletes and develop important relationships. The VIP Lounge should be an immersive environment that invited people to share their experiences online and feature an art gallery.

Design Concepts: Ampersand Studios was transformed using projection mapping and custom content, providing ADIDAS with a home base for their various activations and campaigns throughout the Super Bowl weekend. An AR Moment was created using a free-standing LED wall as a portal for interaction, featuring an AR overlay of the real world with a real-time physics simulation that allowed for interaction with a digital portal. The experience was driven with Interactive programming using TouchDesigner and Motion capture with Microsoft Kinect Azure.

Projection Mapping

Tech: Projection Mapping, Hardware and Software integration, Motion Capture, AR Mirror, Custom Photo booth, Digital Takeaway,

Spec: TouchDesigner, Kinect Azure, Surface Pro, Canon 90D, AI and Computer Vision Algorithms

Partners: KAMP GRIZZLY

Interactive AR Mirror

item.title: Seattle Star: An interactive Lighting Installation
item.content:encoded:
Commissioned by Sortis Holdings in collaboration with Amazon benefiting Mary’s Place homeless shelter, “15 Seconds of Stardom” is an interactive LED Lighting installation that you can control from your phone. By visiting, https://15secondsofstardom.com/ you can design and schedule your own unique light show. After you make a donation you will be able to see your show go live by watching the live stream or waiting for an email that contains a custom video recording of your show after it plays live.

Press: https://www.seattletimes.com/seattle-news/this-christmas-you-can-pay-to-program-the-macys-star-and-brighten-marys-place/

Press: https://www.q13fox.com/video/873228

Type: Interactive LED Lighting Sculpture with Web based Controller

Role: Technical Director: Software, UI/UX, Content Creation, Creative Direction.

Location: Macy’s Place - Seattle, WA

item.title: FLIR History Wall: Interactive Media Display
item.content:encoded:
INTERACTIVE HISTORY WALL

CLIENT: FLIR

LOCATION: Washington, DC

TYPE: Interactive media display

Design Objectives: To showcase FLIR's historic milestones and achievements through an interactive media display that engages and informs the public about the company's technological innovations. The display aims to create a unique experience for visitors at FLIR headquarters in Washington, DC.

Design Concept: Inspired by the Apples' famous springboard app launcher in OSX, the interactive media display uses recognizable "Iron Bow" thermal imaging gradient and LIDAR Scanners to allow participants to control the timeline with their physical body through a touch-free interaction. The display uses AI and computer vision algorithms to parse the sensor data and provide unique IDs for each participant, enabling multiple simultaneous interactions. The screens react to the presence of people passing by and invite them to explore a library of video content and animations related to FLIR's history.

Techniques: Motion Tracking, Video Mapping, Hardware and software integration and design, AI and Computer Vision Algorithms

Materials: Ultra wide format displays, LIDAR Sensors, Media Server, Interactive Software Development

Partners: Imagine[X]

item.title: Aspire: Sculptural Lighting Design
item.content:encoded:
Design Objectives: The 45-foot tall public art installation, Aspire, was commissioned by Louisiana Tech University and the Louisiana Department of Culture, Recreation, and Tourism. The project serves as both an aspirational totem and a symbol of collaboration, unity and inclusivity. It was important to the University that Aspire be both an aspirational totem, as well as a symbol of collaboration, unity, and inclusivity. The art serves to invigorate the urban experience while bringing diverse parts of the campus and surrounding City of Ruston together. A purposeful harmonious relationship was created between the art, the landscape, and the people. This new public space is now a campus focal point and serves as a permanent symbol for humanity.

Design Concept: The project, completed in November 2021, is located at Louisiana Tech University along the east end of the Campus Green. The University has placed a high importance to “welcoming gateways” in the master plan. The historic campus core and new Enterprise Campus come together at this location. Campus leaders have determined this campus confluence would be the perfect location for an iconic work of art. At nearly 45 feet tall, Aspire emerges graciously from the landscape in the form of a cascading fountain. Important to the overall campus master plan, the fountain metaphor is meant to harmonize with another noteworthy public sculpture on campus – the Lady of the Mist.

Type: Public Art, Sculptural Lighting, Permanent Installation

Techniques: Procedural design, electrical engineering, 3D pixel-mapping, generative animation, software development, and lighting design.

Materials: Coated steel pipe, powder coated steel panel, acrylic panel, and integrated linear addressable LED lighting.

Partners: Sclutpture Artist(Curtis Pittman), Night Owl Hal Fabrication

Location: Louisiana Tech University, Ruston, LA

item.title: Bird Of Paradise: Sculptural Lighting Design
item.content:encoded:
Design Objectives: The main objectives of the "Bird of Paradise" sculpture in Carrollwood Village Park, Florida, are to strengthen the park's identity and enhance visitors' connection to the surrounding natural world. The artwork serves as a wayfinding device and iconic symbol for park visitors, and is a tribute to the importance of wildlife and ecological sustainability. It also serves to bring awareness to these issues.

Design Concept: The "Bird of Paradise" sculpture is prominently situated in the SE corner of Carrollwood Village Park, and is a 26-foot-tall tribute to the importance of wildlife and ecological sustainability. It serves as a natural symbol of life and enhances visitors' connection to the surrounding natural world. The sculpture also features animated pixel-mapped LED lighting inside the interior ribs and matching color palette that is highlighted during sunsets.

Type: Public Art, Sculptural Lighting, Permanent Installation

Techniques:  Procedural design, electrical engineering, 3D pixel-mapping, generative animation, software development, and lighting design.

Materials:  Coated steel pipe, powder coated steel panel, Led Controller, ArtNet, DMX, 12V RGB, integrated linear addressable LED lighting

Partners: Sclutpture Artist(Curtis Pittman), Night Owl Hal Fabrication

Location: Carrollwood Village Park, Tampa, Florida

item.title: Centrifuge: Generative Lighting Installation
item.content:encoded:
Lighting Installation at Cedars-Sinai, 6500 Wilshire Boulevard, 20th Floor, FPDC Relocation Los Angeles, CA. Across from the reception wall is a 14-foot-wide by nine-foot-high art installation that consists of 2,500 medical-grade centrifuge tubes protruding from perforated wood panels and backlit by 3,500 color-changing LED lights that animate to represent the city lights. Programmed to an algorithm based on harmonic resonance, the ever-evolving artwork never displays the same color combination twice in 24 hours.

Type: Permanent Lighting Sculpture - Installation

Role: Technical Director - Lighting Design, System Design, Generative Content, Creative Coding, Installation, Integration, Maintenance.

Location: Cedars-Sinai, 6500 Wilshire Boulevard, 20th Floor, FPDC Relocation, Los Angeles, CA

item.title: Converging Influence: 3D Art Tech Wall
item.content:encoded:
Design Objective: The goal of this project was to create an art wall treatment that would converge the core values of the University of Santa Clara with the new science and technology building and curriculum. The mural was designed to be located in the entrance of the state-of-the-art facility, which was designed and managed by ZGF architects. The "Converging Influences" art tech wall was commissioned by the University of Santa Clara's Campus for Innovation and Discovery to create an interactive and visually striking display that unites the core values of the university with the aesthetic and intellectual atmosphere of the campus. The wall was installed on October 15, 2021.

Design Concept: The custom-fabricated 3D wall incorporates LED pixel mapping technology, custom-made software, and generative animated lighting control and playback to create an interactive and visually striking display. The wall was designed with the intention of uniting the core values of the university with the aesthetic and intellectual atmosphere of the Campus for Innovation and Discovery. The mural features a 3D surface that was panelized and milled using Corian and Clear Acrylic. The names of individual donors who contributed to the university are featured in multiple tiers. In addition to the incredible detail and beautiful finishes, embedded individually addressable LED lighting is used throughout to create intrigue. The lighting is pixel-mapped and animated to help convey the concepts of interweaving discovery and innovation. The design of the mural was created using a procedural design approach, allowing for a high level of flexibility and adaptability.

Type: Art Wall, Permanent Lighting Installation, 3D Relief Carving, Mural, Interactive Lighting

Techniques: Procedural Design, Panelization, CNC Milling, Pixel-Mapping, Generative animation, Software Development

Materials: Corian, Acrylic, LED lighting, Steel Frame, Plywood Backboard

Partners: ZGF Architects

item.title: Crystal City: Interactive Model
item.content:encoded:
A commissioned 3D model of Crystal City, VA, in conjunction with the development of the new Amazon headquarters, into an JBG Smith and ZGF wanted an interactive app-controlled light display. The key feature of the installation is a large architectural model, displaying every building in the Crystal City area. Each building sits atop a group of LEDs which light and change the color of the model. A custom control solution was created that dynamically allowed for the grouping and customization of each individual building’s color. Passersby can visually see the current and proposed city by highlighting just the Metro Stations, Parks, or dozens of other categories and buildings.

Type: LED Mapped Architectural Model

Role: Technical Director - System Design, Hardware & Software Integration, Project Management, Software Development, Content Creation, Pixel Mapping, Lighting Design

Location: - JBG Smith Sales Center, Washington DC

A single wall-mounted TV displays further reading and information of the buildings currently selected by the tablet.

Behind the architectural model is a panoramic aerial photo of Crystal City atop more LEDs, creating a glow behind individual buildings in the photo.

item.title: Cameo No 3: Interactive Light Sculpture
item.content:encoded:
Design Objective: To demonstrate the versatility of Dupont's new product Corian® by transforming it into an interactive and immersive artwork that brings together music, touch, light, and social interaction. The objective was to showcase the unique properties of Corian® and how it can be used in innovative ways. The artwork was commissioned as a way to highlight the capabilities of the material and its potential for use in the world of art and design.

Design Concept: Cameo No.3 represents the intersection of music, touch, light, and social interaction, where sound exploration becomes the genesis of art. The piece transforms Dupont's Corian® material into a soft and atmospheric sculpture that allows a play of LED-driven light to glow through, while a multi-zoned sound system plays music through the piece via three touch-activated podiums so that individuals can conduct their experience by way of music and light. The underlying concept is united by the notion of blending advanced materials and technology with nature, warmth and human touch.

Type: Interactive Lighting Sculpture, Audio-Reactive, Semi Permanent

Techniques: The design and fabrication of Cameo No.3 were developed in collaboration with ZGF Architecture and utilized Thermo-formed and CNC routed spires. The piece features a multi-zoned sound system, LED-driven light, and capacitive touch interaction. The sound system is audio reactive, allowing the light and sound to change in response to touch.

Materials: front-milled and thermo-formed acrylic, painted steel pipe, generative animated lighting, generative soundscape, and spatial audio.

Partners: ZGF Architects, Piano Push Play, DotDotDash, DuPont

item.title: Bird Song: Generative Lighting Installation
item.content:encoded:
Design Objectives: The design objectives for Bird Song were to create a permanent lighting installation that served as a wayfinding device and iconic symbol for guests of The Nines, a luxury hotel in Portland, Oregon. The artwork was intended to be a tribute to wildlife and ecological sustainability, and to bring awareness to these issues through its unique form and the use of generative soundscapes incorporating Pacific Northwest bird chirps. Additionally, the installation was designed to be interactive, allowing The Nines hotel to change the color, chirp frequency, volume, and randomness of the lights and soundscapes through a custom iPad application.

Design Concept: The design concept for Bird Song is centered around the idea of representing sound physically through the use of 35-foot glass blown sculptural strands modeled after the sonogram of a repeated bird song. The sculpture is hung with aircraft cable through the middle of each section of hand-blown glass and custom clips hold 3-foot sections of glass in place. LED strips were fabricated to allow double-sided emission and extend voltage transmission length, and addressable lighting technology was used to animate the full vertical height of the chandelier. The iPad control system communicates using OSC over the local network and is remotely controllable.

Type: Interactive Lighting, Audio Reactive Lighting, Permanent Installation

Technique: generative soundscapes, 3D modeling, custom fabrication, animated LED lighting, pixel mapping, and software development

Materials: glass blown sculptural strands, aircraft cable, Laser cut stainless, addressable LED strips, iPad

Location: The Nines Hotel - Portland, Oregon.

Partners: Melody Owen, DotDotDash

[caption id="" align="alignnone" width="1818"] An iPad application was created to give the facility administrators complete control over the sculpture, allowing them to set the mood with color, chirp frequency, volume, and randomness. [/caption]

item.title: Wave: Interactive Light Sculpture
item.content:encoded:
Commissioned by Toyota and SXSW: Fader Fort, “Wave” was an evolving, audio reactive LED sculpture designed to abstractly represent a sound wave. A microphone in front of the sculpture captured participants voice input, resulting in animated content corresponding to frequency displayed across the piece.

Type: Interactive Audio Reactive LED Lighting Sclupture

Role: Technical Director: Programming, Content creation, Fabrication, Design

Location: SXSW, Fader Fort, Austin, TX

item.title: Services
item.content:encoded:
We offer the full spectrum of experiential event design and production services.

Our Services

  • Environmental Design

    Lighting Design

    Pre-Visualization

    3D Animation and Motion Capture

    Realtime Content(AR,VR,XR)

    Audio, Video, and Lighting Content Design

    Consultation and System Design

    Interactive UI / UX Design

  • Custom Fabrication

    Set and Scenic Assembly

    Staging and Rigging

    Lighting Integration

    Trade and Convention Booths

    Permanent Installation

    CNC Machining

    Materials Sourcing and Selection

  • Technical Direction

    Drafting and Detail Drawings

    Engineering Certification

    Software Development

    Site and Floor Plans

    Technical Documentation

    Media Server and Playback Solutions

    AV System Integration

  • Event Planning and Logistics

    AV Equipment Sourcing and Rentals

    Tour Management

    Production Management

    Labor Hire Roles: TD, PM, A1 A2, V1 V2, L1, L2

    Venue Sourcing and Booking

    Permit Submittal

    System Maintenance and Support

Book a Consultation

Our team of technical and creative directors can help you with your project. We specialize in bring unique and bespoke ideas to life. We know that every project is different and we can a tailor custom solution that works for you and your budget. Book a discovery call and get professional advice today.

item.title: DJ Equipment Rentals in Portland | Beatrox
item.content:encoded:
DJ Equipment Rentals

Elevate Your Events with Professional DJ Equipment in Portland, Oregon

Welcome to Beatrox, your premier source for DJ equipment rentals in Portland. Whether you’re planning a concert, wedding, or corporate event, our high-quality equipment and experienced team ensure your event sounds its best.

Our Equipment Includes

Turntables

We carry the latest dj gear with industry standards like the Technics 1200 turntables. We can provide the full Turntablist setup including stylust, needles, slipmats and control vinyl.

CDJs and DJ Controllers

We carry the full line up of Pioneer DJ Equipment, Including the latest Pioneer CDJ-3000, DJM A9, S11. We also have older setup like the CDJ 2000NXS2 and DJM 900NXS2. All of our gear is maintained to the highest standards.

Sound, Lighting, and VIdeo

We have as full suite of of Audio, Video, and Lighting solutions from compact to concert-grade. We work with event planners and producers, mobile djs and bands, venues and conventions, agencies and brands.

Why Choose Beatrox?

  • Quality and Reliability: Our equipment is well-maintained and updated.

  • Expert Support: Our experienced technicians are here to help you find the perfect setup and ensure everything runs smoothly. 20 Years of Experience in Event Production.

  • Competitive Pricing: We offer flexible rental rates that fit your budget.

Ready to start planning your event? Contact us today. Let’s make your next event unforgettable!

item.title: Sound Equipment Rentals
item.content:encoded:
Sound Equipment Rentals

Professional Sound Equipment Rentals for Every Event in Portland

Whether you're hosting a concert, corporate event, or private party, Beatrox offers a comprehensive range of professional sound equipment rentals in Portland. Our top-quality audio gear ensures your event sounds spectacular.

Our Sound Equipment Includes:

Speakers & Live Sound System

Choose from a variety of sizes and power outputs to match your event's needs. With option from RCF and QSC. Our systems are picked for their easy setup and deployment with of the box great sound. Our team will pair you with the appropriate solutions to meet your needs.

Mixers

High-performance mixers suitable for any event type, live performances, exhibition, presentations, conferences, and mobile djs.

Microphones

Wired and wireless handheld microphone options to cater to both speaking engagements and musical performances. We carry the full range of live and studio microphone options.

DJ Equipment

High-performance mixers suitable for any event type, from small gatherings to large-scale productions.

Why Choose Beatrox for Sound Equipment Rentals?

  • Customized Solutions: We tailor our equipment selections to fit the unique requirements of your event.

  • Full Support: Our team provides comprehensive support from setup to breakdown.

  • Competitive Rates: Get the best value with our competitive pricing and exceptional service.

Ready to start planning your event? Contact us today. Let’s make your next event unforgettable!

item.title: Laser Shows
item.content:encoded:
Laser Shows and Effects

Elevate Your Event with Professional Laser Shows from Beatrox

At Beatrox, we specialize in delivering breathtaking laser show displays and effects at unique locations worldwide. Our full-service approach ensures that every aspect of the laser show is meticulously planned and executed, complying with all safety and legal requirements.

Equipment Offered by Beatrox

Our top-of-the-line equipment, powered by Laseronics, ensures that your event's visual impact is nothing short of spectacular.

Laser Projectors

High-quality projectors that provide the backbone for any laser show. We carry the. latest from Laseronics and X-Laser. With a range of outputs from 5W to 30W RGB Lasers.

Custom Laser Displays

Tailored to your specific desires and budget. We will work with you to make sure your audience and clients are blown away.

Aerial Beam Laser Shows

We know how to make your event sizzle with High intensity laser beam effects that will leave your audience stunned.

Laser Graphics

Detailed and dynamic graphic displays for storytelling and thematic presentations. We can make your brand come to life with custom animations and unforgettable displays.

Comprehensive Laser Show Services Include:

Full Service Planning

Including all necessary federal permissions and safety compliance.

Regulatory Compliance

All equipment meets the FDA/CDRH requirements, and we hold the necessary New York “Laser Operator License”.

Safety and Compliance Commitments:

Compliance Services

Adhering to FDA/CDRH and FAA regulations to ensure safety and legality.

Certified Expertise

Every show is managed by a certified Laser Safety Officer (LSO) with required federal variances.

Aviation Coordination

Secure FAA permissions for outdoor laser shows.

Why Choose Beatrox and Laseronics for Your Laser Shows?

  • Global Reach: Extensive experience in delivering shows worldwide.

  • Professional Recognition: Proud members of ILDA and winners of multiple awards, adhering to a strict code of ethics.

  • Advanced Technology: Utilizing state-of-the-art Pangolin Laser Software for unmatched precision in live and time-coded shows.

Ready to start planning your event? Contact us today. Let’s make your next event unforgettable!

item.title: Drone Light Shows
item.content:encoded:
Drone Light Shows

Experience the Magic of Drone Light Shows with Beatrox

Transform your event into a breathtaking spectacle with Beatrox’s Drone Light Shows. Beatrox, powered by Sky Lites Drone Shows, brings innovative aerial displays that blend advanced drone technology with creative lighting and multimedia effects.

Our Drone Light Show Services Include:

Customized Drone Light Displays

Tailor your drone show to fit the theme of your event, from corporate launches to private weddings.

Multimedia Integration

Combine drones with lasers and customized soundtracks to create a truly immersive experience.

Advanced Technology

Utilize GPS tracking and RTK satellite communications to ensure precise and safe performances.

Drone Light Show Animation

We have a team of talented 3D animators ready to make your creative visions come to life as a drone light show in the sky!

Comprehensive Support:

Full Service Planning

We handle everything from initial concepts to post-event reviews, ensuring your satisfaction at every stage.

Professional Team

Our diverse crew includes animators, technicians, and safety experts who are committed to delivering exceptional results.

Why Choose Beatrox for Your Drone Light Shows?

  • Expertise and Innovation: Powered by Sky Lites, our team has been at the forefront of the light show industry since its inception.

  • Complete Customization: From choreography to music and lighting effects, every aspect of the show is customized to meet your needs.

  • Safety and Compliance: Our team includes FAA-certified pilots and compliance officers, ensuring all shows are executed with the highest safety standards.

  • IFO Drones: Equipped with bright LED lights programmed for high-impact visual displays

  • Laser FX Integration: Enhance your drone shows with additional laser effects for a spectacular light show.

  • Global Reach: Based in the USA, we serve clients worldwide, adapting to a variety of international regulations and venues.

Ready to Light Up the Sky?

Whether you’re planning a local event or a global spectacle, Contact us today to learn how our Drone Light Shows can make your event unforgettable. Let Beatrox and Sky Lites take your celebration to new heights!

item.title: Infinite Playlist Tour: Festival Activation - Amazon Music Live at Outside Lands 2023
item.content:encoded:
IFINITE PLAYLIST TOUR

CLIENT: Amazon Music Live

LOCATION: Outside Lands Music Festival 2023

TYPE: Interactive Media Experience, Event Production, Festival Activation

Design Objectives: Amazon Music aimed to create an unforgettable festival experience that would break through the clutter and delight audiences at select 2023 music festivals. The objective was to provide fans with unprecedented access to music and artists they love, while also engaging them with interactive elements that enhance their festival experience.

Design Concept :At Outside Lands, the experience featured a reflective exterior and a custom mural by Bay Area artist Chaz Bear. Fans passed through a colorful, astera-lit hallway into a music-filled space that took over the 3-day festival. The space included meet & greet sessions with artists and interviews hosted by Bimma Williams, enhancing fan engagement. At Stagecoach, attendees enjoyed the official festival livestream in a striking, technicolor build with interactive touchpoints throughout the space, including exclusive merch giveaways, artist meet & greets, VIP pass giveaways, and an Amazon Music LIVE photobooth.

Type: Interactive Media Experience, Event Production, Festival Activation

Techniques: Reflective Exterior Design, Custom Mural Creation, Animated LED Lighting, Interactive Touchpoints, AR Moments with Free-standing LED Walls, Real-time Physics Simulations, Interactive Programming

Materials:  LED Video Panels, Reflective Materials, Astera Lights, Custom Murals, TouchDesigner Software, Microsoft Kinect Azure

Partners: Kamp Grizzly, Hawthorne Industries, Sharing Box

IFINITE PLAYLIST TOUR

CLIENT: Amazon Music Live

LOCATION: Stage Coach

TYPE: Interactive Media Experience, Event Production, Festival Activation

item.title: LED Video Wall Rentals
item.content:encoded:
LED VIDEO WALL

Transform Your Events with High-Resolution LED Video Walls from Beatrox

Make a visual impact at your next event with Beatrox’s state-of-the-art LED video walls. We offer a variety of product solutions with panels perfect for any setting, from outdoor concerts to studio stages and special events. Based in Portland, we offer tailored solutions that cater to the unique needs of each client.

Our LED Video Wall Services Include:

Customized Configurations

Whether you need a ground-supported freestanding setup or an elevated and flown deployment, our team can handle all arrangements.

Comprehenzive Planning and Support

We provide complete planning services, from initial concept to final execution, ensuring your video wall display meets all your event requirements.

Multimedia Integration

Combine Large format video displays with stunning custom visual content into fabricated scenic and set pieces to create a truly immersive experience.

Media Playback Solutions

We provide solutions for video playback, weather your application demands realtime generative content, live streams, timecode playback, or just a single video, we have go you covered.

Full Service Planning

We handle everything from initial concepts and layouts to detailed technical drawings and post-event reviews, ensuring your satisfaction at every stage.

Professional Team

Our diverse crew includes video content creators, video technicians, production coordinators, and safety experts who are committed to delivering exceptional results.

Why Choose Beatrox for LED Video Wall Rentals?

  • Expertise in Visual Technology: With years of experience in providing cutting-edge visual solutions, Beatrox is your trusted partner in enhancing event experiences.

  • Tailored Solutions for Every Event: Our team works closely with you to design a video wall setup that perfectly matches the scale and theme of your event.

  • Commitment to Quality and Service: We ensure every aspect of our service meets high standards, from the quality of our equipment to customer support and technical assistance.

Ready to Add a Stunning Visual Element to Your Event?

For events in Portland and beyond, contact Beatrox today to discuss your LED video wall needs. Let us help you create an unforgettable visual experience for your audience!
